evals.name: saas-marketing-site
evals.prompt: I have a marketing website for my SaaS product. We capture signups, track page views, and have CRM data from HubSpot. 53 solution pages, a blog, and landing pages. Every page is completely static — same copy for every visitor. How should I add personalization using Personize?
evals.expected_output: Proposals that center on AI-generating page copy, blog intros, and CTAs using governance + unified memory + prompt instructions. Not template swaps or tag-based branching.

assertions.name: uses_all_three_layers
assertions.description: Every proposal explicitly uses governance (smartGuidelines), unified memory (smartDigest/recall), AND content generation (prompt with instructions[])

assertions.name: generates_content_not_tags
assertions.description: Proposals center on AI WRITING original content (paragraphs, headlines, copy) — not tagging users or swapping predefined templates

assertions.name: passes_differentiation_test
assertions.description: Each proposal could NOT be done with just a CRM + basic LLM call. Requires cross-source context + governance + multi-step generation.

assertions.name: includes_governance_role
assertions.description: Governance is shown as essential (brand voice controlling output, compliance rules) — not optional or mentioned in passing

assertions.name: shows_unified_memory_value
assertions.description: Proposals show how combining data from multiple sources (CRM + analytics + signups) unlocks something impossible with any single source

evals.name: b2b-project-mgmt
evals.prompt: I run a B2B project management tool called TaskFlow. We have user activity data in PostgreSQL, Stripe billing data, and Intercom support tickets. About 2,000 active teams use us. I want to reduce churn and increase feature adoption. What personalization opportunities should I implement with Personize?
evals.expected_output: Proposals for AI-generated health check-ins, feature adoption nudges, and churn prevention messages — all using governance + cross-source memory + multi-step prompt generation.

assertions.name: uses_all_three_layers
assertions.description: Every proposal explicitly uses governance, unified memory, AND content generation

assertions.name: generates_content_not_tags
assertions.description: Proposals center on AI WRITING original content — not tagging or segmenting users

assertions.name: passes_differentiation_test
assertions.description: Each proposal requires Personize specifically — not achievable with basic tools

assertions.name: cross_source_synthesis
assertions.description: Shows how combining PostgreSQL activity + Stripe billing + Intercom tickets creates context no single source has

assertions.name: includes_governance_role
assertions.description: Governance shown as essential — CS playbook, messaging tone, escalation rules

evals.name: ecommerce-scale
evals.prompt: We're an e-commerce company doing about 100K purchases per month on Shopify. We have customer purchase history, browsing behavior analytics, Klaviyo email marketing, and a loyalty program. We want to move beyond basic 'recommended for you' product carousels. How can Personize help us create truly personalized shopping experiences?
evals.expected_output: Proposals for AI-generated product descriptions per visitor, governance-controlled promotional messaging, AI-written post-purchase follow-ups that synthesize purchase + browsing + loyalty data.

assertions.name: uses_all_three_layers
assertions.description: Every proposal explicitly uses governance, unified memory, AND content generation

assertions.name: generates_content_not_tags
assertions.description: Proposals go beyond recommendation engines — AI WRITES content, not just selects products

assertions.name: passes_differentiation_test
assertions.description: Proposals could NOT be done with Klaviyo segments + a basic LLM call

assertions.name: cross_source_synthesis
assertions.description: Shows how combining Shopify purchases + browsing + Klaviyo + loyalty creates unique context

assertions.name: includes_governance_role
assertions.description: Governance controls brand voice, promotional rules, compliance (e.g. discount policies)

evals.name: accounting-consulting
evals.prompt: We're an accounting and consulting services firm selling to SMBs. We have about 500 existing clients in our HubSpot CRM and are actively doing business development to grow. Our team does outbound prospecting, runs email marketing campaigns, and manages ongoing client relationships. How should we use Personize across sales outreach, marketing, and client management?
evals.expected_output: Proposals for AI-written prospecting emails using governance (firm positioning, compliance), AI-generated client check-ins synthesizing CRM + engagement + service history, governance-controlled marketing that follows professional services messaging standards.

assertions.name: uses_all_three_layers
assertions.description: Every proposal explicitly uses governance, unified memory, AND content generation

assertions.name: generates_content_not_tags
assertions.description: Proposals center on AI WRITING outreach, client communications, and marketing — not CRM tagging

assertions.name: passes_differentiation_test
assertions.description: Proposals require Personize — not achievable with HubSpot sequences + a basic LLM call

assertions.name: covers_all_three_areas
assertions.description: Addresses all three requested areas: sales outreach, marketing campaigns, AND client management

assertions.name: includes_governance_role
assertions.description: Governance enforces professional services standards — firm positioning, regulatory compliance, messaging consistency across partners
